explanation: The contract performs an external call or grows storage (array .push) inside a loop. Gas cost scales with the number of iterations, so once the collection is large enough the transaction exceeds the block gas limit and the function becomes permanently uncallable. A loop of external calls is doubly dangerous: one reverting or gas guzzling recipient (a contract with a hostile fallback) makes the whole batch fail for everyone.
impact: An attacker can grow the list (or register one malicious recipient that always reverts) to make the loop exceed the block gas limit or fail mid batch, permanently blocking payouts and freezing funds for all users, as happened with the GovernMental Ponzi payout loop.
